questions.expression_latex: \text{If } a, b, c \text{ are sides of a triangle, prove that the equation } a^2 x^2 + (b^2 + a^2 - c^2) x + b^2 = 0 \text{ has no real roots.}
questions.question_text: (IIT-JEE classic) Use the law of cosines: b² + a² − c² = 2ab cos C.  Then show D < 0.
questions.answer_latex: D = (b^2 + a^2 - c^2)^2 - 4 a^2 b^2 = (2 a b \cos C)^2 - 4 a^2 b^2 = 4 a^2 b^2 (\cos^2 C - 1) = -4 a^2 b^2 \sin^2 C \leq 0 \text{ (strict < 0 for a non-degenerate triangle)}.
